{\n218\t\t\treturn false\n219\t\t}\n220\t\n221\t\t// Valid custom error types should:\n222\t\t// - Be lowercase\n223\t\t// - Use underscores between words\n224\t\t// - Not contain special characters letters, numbers, underscores)\n232\t\tfor _, c := range errorType {\n233\t\t\tif !((c >= 'a' && c <= 'z') || (c >= '0' && c <= '9') || c == '_') {\n234\t\t\t\treturn result.\n82\tfunc (r SuccessResponseMatchResult) String() string {\n83\t\tif r.Success {\n84\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Success response validation passed: status %d, content-type %s\",\n85\t\t\t\tr.StatusCode, r.ContentType)\n86\t\t}\n87\t\treturn r.Error\n88\t}\n89\t\n90\t// ValidateSuccessResponseDetailed performs comprehensive success response validation.\n91\t//\n92\t// This function validates all aspects of a successful HTTP response:\n93\t// - Status code is in 2xx range\n94\t// - Content-Type header matches expected type\n95\t// - Response body structure matches expected format\n96\t// - Data types match schema\n97\t// - Required fields are present\n98\t//\n99\t// Parameters:\n100\t//   - response: The HTTP response to validate (*httptest.ResponseRecorder or ValidateSuccessResponseDetailed(response interface{}, expectedContentType string, expectedStructure map[string]string, requiredFields []string) SuccessResponseMatchResult {\n116\t\tresult := true\n173\t\t}\n174\t\n175\t\t// Validate required fields\n176\t\tif len(requiredFields) > 0 {\n177\t\t\trequiredFieldsResult := ValidateSuccessRequiredFields(responseBody, append(result.RequiredFieldErrors, requiredFieldsResult.Errors...)\n181\t\t\t}\n182\t\t} else {\n183\t\t\tresult.RequiredFieldsValidation = true\n184\t\t}\n185\t\n186\t\t// Build overall success &&\n191\t\t\tresult.ContentTypeValidation\n192\t\n193\t\t// Build error message if validation failed\n194\t\tif !result.Success {\n195\t\t\tresult.Error =  - SuccessStructureResult with structure validation details\n209\t//\n210\t// Example:\n211\t//   expected := map[string]string{\"id\": \"string\", \"count\": \"number\", \"active\": \"boolean\"}\n212\t//   result := ValidateSuccessStructure(responseBody, expected)\n213\tfunc ValidateSuccessStructure(responseBody []byte, expectedStructure map[string]string) SuccessStructureResult {\n221\t\t\tresult.Success = false\n222\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, \"Response body is empty\")\n223\t\t\treturn result\n224\t\t}\n225\t\n226\t\t// Parse JSON body\n227\t\tvar body append(result.Errors,\n231\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse response body as JSON: %v\", err))\n232\t\t\treturn result\n233\t\t}\n234\t\n235\t\t// Check for expected fields\n236\t\tfor fieldName, validation)\n245\t\t// This can be useful to catch API changes or version mismatches\n246\t\t// but we'll make it lenient for now\n247\t\n248\t\treturn result\n249\t}\n250\t\n251\t// ValidateSuccessDataTypes validates response data types match expected schema.\n252\t//\n253\t// Parameters:\n254\t//   - responseBody: The response body as bytes (typically JSON)\n255\t//   - expectedTypes: Map of field names to expected type descriptions\n256\t//\n257\t// Returns:\n258\t//   - SuccessDataTypeResult with data type validation details\n259\t//\n260\t// Example:\n261\t// ValidateSuccessDataTypes(responseBody []byte, expectedTypes map[string]string) SuccessDataTypeResult {\n264\t\tresult := SuccessDataTypeResult{\n265\t\t\tSuccess: true,\n266\t\t\tErrors:  []string{},\n267\t\t}\n268\t\n269\t\t// Parse JSON body\n270\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n271\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(responseBody, &body); err != nil {\n272\t\t\tresult.Success = types for each field\n279\t\tfor fieldName, expectedType := range expectedTypes {\n280\t\t\tvalue, exists := body[fieldName]\n281\t\t\tif !exists {\n282\t\t\t\tcontinue // Skip missing fields (handled by required fields validation)\n283\t\t\t}\n284\t\n285\t\t\t// Skip null values - they're allowed for non-required fields\n286\t\t\tif value == nil {\n287\t\t\t\tcontinue\n288\t\t\t}\n289\t\n290\t\t\tactualType := getDataType(value)\n291\t\t\tif !typesMatch(expectedType, actualType) {\n292\t\t\t\tresult.Success = false\n293\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors,\n294\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Type mismatch for field '%s': expected %s, got %s\",\n295\t\t\t\t\t\tfieldName, expectedType, actualType))\n296\t\t\t}\n297\t\t}\n298\t\n299\t\treturn ValidateSuccessRequiredFields(responseBody []byte, requiredFields []string) SuccessRequiredFieldsResult {\n315\t\tresult := SuccessRequiredFieldsResult{\n316\t\t\tSuccess:         true,\n317\t\t\tMissingFields:   []string{},\n318\t\t\tPresentFields:  []string{},\n319\t\t\tErrors:         []string{},\n320\t\t}\n321\t\n322\t\t// Parse JSON body\n323\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n324\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(responseBody, &body); err != nil {\n325\t\t\tresult.Success = false\n326\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors,\n327\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse response body as JSON: %v\", err))\n328\t\t\treturn result\n329\t\t}\n330\t\n331\t\t// Check for required fields\n332\t\tfor _, field := range requiredFields {\n333\t\t\tif _, exists := body[field]; exists {\n334\t\t\t\tresult.PresentFields = append(result.PresentFields, field)\n335\t\t\t} else {\n336\t\t\t\tresult.Success = false\n337\t\t\t\tresult.MissingFields = append(result.MissingFields, field)\n338\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors,\n339\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Missing required field: %s\", field))\n340\t\t\t}\n341\t\t}\n342\t\n343\t\treturn result\n344\t}\n345\t\n346\t// ValidateSuccessContentType validates content-type for success responses.\n347\t//\n348\t// Parameters:\n349\t//   - details\n354\t//\n355\t// Example:\n356\t//   result := ValidateSuccessContentType(w, \"application/json\")\n357\tfunc ValidateSuccessContentType(response interface{}, expectedContentType string) SuccessContentTypeResult {\n358\t\tresult := SuccessContentTypeResult{\n359\t\t\tSuccess: true,\n360\t\t\tErrors:  []string{},\n361\t\t}\n362\t\n363\t\tactualContentType := getContentType(response)\n364\t\n365\t\t// Validate status code is success (2xx)\n366\t\tstatusCode := getStatusCode(response)\n367\t\tif !isSuccessStatusCode(statusCode) {\n368\t\t\tresult.Success = false\n369\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors,\n370\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is not in 2xx success range\", statusCode))\n371\t\t}\n372\t\n373\t\t// Validate content-type\n374\t\tif !contentTypeMatches(actualContentType, expectedContentType) {\n375\t\t\tresult.Success = false\n376\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors,\n377\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Content-Type mismatch: expected %s, got %s\",\n378\t\t\t\t\texpectedContentType, actualContentType))\n379\t\t}\n380\t\n381\t\tresult.ExpectedContentType = expectedContentType\n382\t\tresult.ActualContentType = actualContentType\n383\t\tresult.StatusCode = statusCode\n384\t\n385\t\treturn result\n386\t}\n387\t\n388\t// ValidateSuccessJSONSchema validates response body against JSON schema.\n389\t//\n390\t// This is a basic schema validation implementation. For production use,\n391\t// consider integrating with a full JSON schema validation library.\n392\t//\n393\t// Parameters:\n394\t//   - responseBody: The response body as bytes (typically JSON)\n395\t//   - schema: Basic schema definition (map of field names to type requirements)\n396\t//\n397\t// Returns:\n398\t//   - SuccessSchemaResult with schema validation details\n399\t//\n400\t// Example:\n401\t//   schema := map[string]interface{}{\n402\t//       \"id\": map[string]string{\"type\": \"string\", \"required\": \"true\"},\n403\t//       \"name\": map[string]string{\"type\": \"string\", \"required\": \"true\"},\n404\t//       \"age\": map[string]string{\"type\": \"number\", \"required\": \"false\"},\n405\t//   }\n406\t//   result := ValidateSuccessJSONSchema(responseBody, schema)\n407\tfunc ValidateSuccessJSONSchema(responseBody []byte, schema map[string]interface{}) SuccessSchemaResult {\n408\t\tresult := SuccessSchemaResult{\n409\t\t\tSuccess: true,\n410\t\t\tErrors:  []string{},\n411\t\t}\n412\t\n413\t\t// Parse JSON body\n414\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n415\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(responseBody, &body); err != nil {\n416\t\t\tresult.Success = false\n417\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors,\n418\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse response body as JSON: %v\", err))\n419\t\t\treturn result\n420\t\t}\n421\t\n422\t\t// Validate schema requirements\n423\t\tfor fieldName, schemaDef := range schema {\n424\t\t\tschemaMap, ok := schemaDef.(map[string]string)\n425\t\t\tif !ok {\n426\t\t\t\tcontinue\n427\t\t\t}\n428\t\n429\t\t\t// Check if field exists\n430\t\t\tvalue, exists := body[fieldName]\n431\t\t\tif !exists {\n432\t\t\t\t// Check if required\n433\t\t\t\tif required, ok := schemaMap[\"required\"]; ok && required == \"true\" {\n434\t\t\t\t\tresult.Success = false\n435\t\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors,\n436\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Missing required field: %s\", fieldName))\n437\t\t\t\t}\n438\t\t\t\tcontinue\n439\t\t\t}\n440\t\n441\t\t\t// Skip null values - they're allowed for non-required fields\n442\t\t\tif value == nil {\n443\t\t\t\tcontinue\n444\t\t\t}\n445\t\n446\t\t\t// Validate type if specified\n447\t\t\tif expectedType, ok := schemaMap[\"type\"]; ok {\n448\t\t\t\tactualType := getDataType(value)\n449\t\t\t\tif !typesMatch(expectedType, actualType) {\n450\t\t\t\t\tresult.Success = false\n451\t\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors,\n452\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Type mismatch for field '%s': expected %s, got %s\",\n453\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfieldName, expectedType, actualType))\n454\t\t\t\t}\n455\t\t\t}\n456\t\t}\n457\t\n458\t\treturn result\n459\t}\n460\t\n461\t// =============================================================================\n462\t// RESULT TYPES\n463\t// =============================================================================\n464\t\n465\t// SuccessStructureResult contains the result of structure validation.\n466\ttype SuccessStructureResult struct {\n467\t\tSuccess bool\n468\t\tErrors  []string\n469\t}\n470\t\n471\t// SuccessDataTypeResult contains the result of data type validation.\n472\ttype SuccessDataTypeResult struct {\n473\t\tSuccess bool\n474\t\tErrors  []string\n475\t}\n476\t\n477\t// SuccessRequiredFieldsResult contains the result of required fields validation.\n478\ttype SuccessRequiredFieldsResult struct {\n479\t\tSuccess        bool\n480\t\tMissingFields  []string\n481\t\tPresentFields  []string\n482\t\tErrors         []string\n483\t}\n484\t\n485\t// SuccessContentTypeResult contains the result of content-type validation.\n486\ttype SuccessContentTypeResult struct {\n487\t\tSuccess             bool\n488\t\tStatusCode          int\n489\t\tExpectedContentType string\n490\t\tActualContentType   string\n491\t\tErrors              []string\n492\t}\n493\t\n494\t// SuccessSchemaResult contains the result of JSON schema validation.\n495\ttype SuccessSchemaResult struct {\n496\t\tSuccess bool\n497\t\tErrors  []string\n498\t}\n499\t\n500\t// =============================================================================\n501\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n502\t// =============================================================================\n503\t\n504\t// isSuccessStatusCode checks if a status code is in the 2xx success range.\n505\tfunc isSuccessStatusCode(statusCode int) bool {\n506\t\treturn statusCode >= 200 && statusCode < 300\n507\t}\n508\t\n509\t// getDataType returns the data type of a value.\n510\tfunc getDataType(value interface{}) string {\n511\t\tswitch value.(type) {\n512\t\tcase string:\n513\t\t\treturn \"string\"\n514\t\tcase int, int8, int16, int32, int64, uint, uint8, uint16, uint32, uint64:\n515\t\t\treturn \"number\"\n516\t\tcase float32, float64:\n517\t\t\treturn \"number\"\n518\t\tcase bool:\n519\t\t\treturn \"boolean\"\n520\t\tcase []interface{}:\n521\t\t\treturn \"array\"\n522\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n523\t\t\treturn \"object\"\n524\t\tcase nil:\n525\t\t\treturn \"null\"\n526\t\tdefault:\n527\t\t\treturn \"unknown\"\n528\t\t}\n529\t}\n530\t\n531\t// typesMatch checks if expected and actual types match.\n532\tfunc typesMatch(expected, actual string) bool {\n533\t\t// Handle flexible type matching\n534\t\tswitch expected {\n535\t\tcase \"number\":\n536\t\t\treturn actual == \"number\" || actual == \"integer\"\n537\t\tcase \"integer\":\n538\t\t\treturn actual == \"number\" || actual == \"integer\"\n539\t\tcase \"string\":\n540\t\t\treturn actual == \"string\"\n541\t\tcase \"boolean\":\n542\t\t\treturn actual == \"boolean\"\n543\t\tcase \"array\":\n544\t\t\treturn actual == \"array\"\n545\t\tcase \"object\":\n546\t\t\treturn actual == \"object\"\n547\t\tdefault:\n548\t\t\treturn expected == actual\n549\t\t}\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\t// getResponseBody extracts response body from response object.\n553\tfunc getResponseBody(response interface{}) []byte {\n554\t\tswitch r := response.(type) {\n555\t\tcase *httptest.ResponseRecorder:\n556\t\t\treturn r.Body.Bytes()\n557\t\tcase *http.Response:\n558\t\t\tif r.Body != nil []byte{}\n566\t\t}\n567\t}\n568\t\n569\t\n570\t// buildSuccessValidationError builds a comprehensive error message from validation result.\n571\tfunc buildSuccessValidationError(result =============================================================================\n// SUCCESS RESPONSE VALIDATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n// This module provides comprehensive success response validation utilities\n// for validating successful HTTP responses (2xx status codes). It supports\n// structure validation, data format/type checking, schema validation, required\n// field checking, and content-type validation for success responses.\n//\n// Functions:\n// - ValidateSuccessResponse: Validates complete success response (status, structure, content-type)\n// - ValidateSuccessStructure: Validates response body structure matches expected format\n// - ValidateSuccessDataTypes: Validates response data types match schema\n// - ValidateSuccessRequiredFields: Validates required success fields are present\n// - ValidateSuccessContentType: Validates GetSuccessFieldTypes: Returns expected data types for common success response fields\n// - GetRequiredSuccessFields: Returns required fields for common success response types\n// =============================================================================\n\n// SuccessResponseMatchResult contains detailed information about a success response validation.\n//\n// This type provides comprehensive error context for debugging validation failures,\n// including whether each validation aspect passed, the response object context,\n// and formatted error messages for any failures.\ntype SuccessResponseMatchResult struct {\n\t// Success indicates whether all validations passed successfully\n\tSuccess bool\n\n\t// StatusCodeValidation indicates whether status code validation passed\n\tStatusCodeValidation bool\n\n\t// StructureValidation indicates whether response structure validation passed\n\tStructureValidation bool\n\n\t// DataTypeValidation indicates whether data type validation passed\n\tDataTypeValidation bool\n\n\t// RequiredFieldsValidation indicates whether required fields validation passed\n\tRequiredFieldsValidation bool\n\n\t// ContentTypeValidation indicates whether content-type validation passed\n\tContentTypeValidation bool\n\n\t// ResponseContext contains information about the response object\n\tResponseContext string\n\n\t// StatusCode is the actual status code received\n\tStatusCode int\n\n\t// ContentType is the actual content-type received\n\tContentType string\n\n\t// StructureErrors contains any structure validation errors\n\tStructureErrors []string\n\n\t// DataTypeErrors contains any data type validation errors\n\tDataTypeErrors []string\n\n\t// RequiredFieldErrors contains any required field validation errors\n\tRequiredFieldErrors []string\n\n\t// ContentTypeErrors contains any content-type validation errors\n\tContentTypeErrors []string\n\n\t// Error is a formatted error message := json.Unmarshal(responseBody, &body); err != nil {\n\t\tresult.Success = false\n\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors,\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse response body as JSON: %v\", err))\n\t\treturn result\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate schema requirements\n\tfor fieldName, schemaDef := range schema {\n\t\tschemaMap, ok := schemaDef.(map[string]string)\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check if field exists\n\t\tvalue, exists := body[fieldName]\n\t\tif !exists {\n\t\t\t// Check if required\n\t\t\tif required, ok := schemaMap[\"required\"]; ok && required == \"true\" {\n\t\t\t\tresult.Success = false\n\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors,\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Missing required field: %s\", fieldName))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Skip null values - they're allowed for non-required fields\n\t\tif value == nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Validate type if specified\n\t\tif expectedType, ok := schemaMap[\"type\"]; ok {\n\t\t\tactualType := getDataType(value)\n\t\t\tif !typesMatch(expectedType, actualType) {\n\t\t\t\tresult.Success = false\n\t\t\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors,\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Type mismatch for field '%s': expected %s, got %s\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfieldName, expectedType, actualType))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// RESULT TYPES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// SuccessStructureResult contains the result of structure validation.\ntype SuccessStructureResult struct {\n\tSuccess bool\n\tErrors  []string\n}\n\n// SuccessDataTypeResult contains the result of data type validation.\ntype SuccessDataTypeResult struct {\n\tSuccess bool\n\tErrors  []string\n}\n\n// SuccessRequiredFieldsResult contains the result of required fields validation.\ntype SuccessRequiredFieldsResult struct {\n\tSuccess        bool\n\tMissingFields  []string\n\tPresentFields  []string\n\tErrors         []string\n}\n\n// SuccessContentTypeResult contains the result of content-type validation.\ntype SuccessContentTypeResult struct {\n\tSuccess             bool\n\tStatusCode          int\n\tExpectedContentType string\n\tActualContentType   string\n\tErrors              []string\n}\n\n// SuccessSchemaResult contains the result of JSON schema validation.\ntype SuccessSchemaResult struct {\n\tSuccess bool\n\tErrors  []string\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// isSuccessStatusCode checks if a status code is in the 2xx success range.\nfunc isSuccessStatusCode(statusCode int) bool {\n\treturn statusCode >= 200 && statusCode < 300\n}\n\n// getDataType returns the data type of a value.\nfunc getDataType(value interface{}) string {\n\tswitch value.(type) {\n\tcase string:\n\t\treturn \"string\"\n\tcase int, int8, int16, int32, int64, uint, uint8, uint16, uint32, uint64:\n\t\treturn \"number\"\n\tcase float32, float64:\n\t\treturn \"number\"\n\tcase bool:\n\t\treturn \"boolean\"\n\tcase []interface{}:\n\t\treturn \"array\"\n\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n\t\treturn \"object\"\n\tcase nil:\n\t\treturn \"null\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"unknown\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// typesMatch checks if expected and actual types match.\nfunc typesMatch(expected, actual string) bool {\n\t// Handle flexible type matching\n\tswitch expected {\n\tcase \"number\":\n\t\treturn actual == \"number\" || actual == \"integer\"\n\tcase \"integer\":\n\t\treturn actual == \"number\" || actual == \"integer\"\n\tcase \"string\":\n\t\treturn actual == \"string\"\n\tcase \"boolean\":\n\t\treturn actual == \"boolean\"\n\tcase \"array\":\n\t\treturn actual == \"array\"\n\tcase \"object\":\n\t\treturn actual == \"object\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn expected == actual\n\t}\n}\n\n// getResponseBody extracts response body from response object.\nfunc getResponseBody(response interface{}) []byte {\n\tswitch r := response.(type) {\n\tcase *httptest.ResponseRecorder:\n\t\treturn r.Body.Bytes()\n\tcase *http.Response:\n\t\tif r.Body != nil {\n\t\t\tdefer r.Body.Close()\n\t\t\tbodyBytes, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\t\t\treturn bodyBytes\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn []byte{}\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn []byte{}\n\t}\n}\n\n\n// buildSuccessValidationError builds a comprehensive error message from validation result.\nfunc buildSuccessValidationError(result SuccessResponseMatchResult) string {\n\tvar errors []string\n\n\tif !result.StatusCodeValidation {\n\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is not in 2xx success range\", result.StatusCode))\n\t}\n\n\tif !result.ContentTypeValidation && len(result.ContentTypeErrors) > 0 {\n\t\terrors = append(errors, result.ContentTypeErrors...)\n\t}\n\n\tif !result.StructureValidation && len(result.StructureErrors) > 0 {\n\t\terrors = append(errors, result.StructureErrors...)\n\t}\n\n\tif !result.DataTypeValidation && len(result.DataTypeErrors) > 0 {\n\t\terrors = append(errors, result.DataTypeErrors...)\n\t}\n\n\tif !result.RequiredFieldsValidation && len(result.RequiredFieldErrors) > 0 {\n\t\terrors = append(errors, result.RequiredFieldErrors...)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Success response validation failed:\\n  %s\",\n\t\tstrings.Join(errors, \"\\n  \"))\n}\n\n// response (e.g., \"create\", \"update\", \"list\")\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - List of required field names for the specified response type\n//\n// Example:\n//   required := \"number\"},\n\t\t{\"bool\", true, \"boolean\"},\n\t\t{\"array\", []interface{}{1, 2, 3}, \"array\"},\n\t\t{\"object\", map[string]interface{}{\"key\": \"value\"}, \"object\"},\n\t\t{\"nil\", nil, \"null\"},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := getDataType(tt.value)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected type %s for %v, got %s\", tt.expected, tt.value, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestTypesMatch tests type matching logic.\nfunc TestTypesMatch(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\texpected string\n\t\tactual   string\n\t\texpectedMatch bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"string match\", \"string\", \"string\", true},\n\t\t{\"boolean match\", \"boolean\", \"boolean\", \"string\", false},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := typesMatch(tt.expected, tt.actual)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expectedMatch GetRequiredSuccessFields(tt.responseType)\n\t\t\tif len(result) != len(tt.expectedFields) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected %d required fields for %s, got %d\",\n\t\t\t\t\tlen(tt.expectedFields), tt.responseType, len(result))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestGetSuccessContentType tests content-type mapping.\nfunc TestGetSuccessContentType(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tresponseType string\n\t\texpected     string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"json\", \"application/json\"},\n\t\t{\"xml\", \"application/xml\"},\n\t\t{\"html\", \"text/html\"},\n\t\t{\"text\", \"text/plain\"},\n\t\t{\"form\", \"application/x-www-form-urlencoded\"},\n\t\t{\"unknown\", \"application/json\"},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.responseType, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := GetSuccessContentType(tt.responseType)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected content-type %s for %s, got %s\",\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.expected, tt.responseType, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestValidateSuccessContentTypePattern tests content-type pattern matching.\nfunc TestValidateSuccessContentTypePattern(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct \"application/xml\", \"application/*\", true},\n\t\t{\"no pattern\", \"application/json\", \"\", true},\n\t\t{\"mismatch\", \"text/html\", \"application/json\", false},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range
